quarantine.oracle: Query results (e.g., number of records, record fields) are invariant under reordering of header records, as header record order is not semantically significant for data queries

evidence.quote: SAM specification does not prescribe a required order for header record types beyond convention; query results should be independent of header ordering

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under SAM-to-BAM-to-SAM round trip conversion, as the format conversion is lossless

evidence.quote: SAM and BAM are equivalent representations; round-trip conversion should preserve all data fields

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under SAM-to-CRAM-to-SAM round trip conversion for losslessly representable data

evidence.quote: CRAM is a compressed representation of SAM data; round-trip conversion should preserve queryable fields

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under permutation of optional tag fields, as tag order within a record is not semantically significant

evidence.quote: Optional tags in SAM records are identified by their two-letter keys, not their positional order

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under case normalization of the SEQ field, as case is not semantically significant in SAM sequences

evidence.quote: SAM SEQ field uses uppercase IUPAC characters; case normalization does not change the biological meaning

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under canonicalization of RNEXT '=' alias to the actual reference sequence name, as '=' is a shorthand notation

evidence.quote: In SAM, RNEXT '=' is an alias for the RNAME of the current record; canonicalization preserves semantics

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under normalization of unmapped record fields, as unmapped records have defined default values for certain fields

evidence.quote: SAM specification defines specific field values for unmapped reads; normalization applies these conventions

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under canonicalization of CIGAR match operators (= vs M), as both represent alignment matches

evidence.quote: CIGAR '=' and 'M' operators both denote alignment matches; canonicalization does not change alignment semantics

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under shuffling of HD header subtags, as subtag order within a header record is not semantically significant

evidence.quote: Header record subtags are key-value pairs; their order within a header line is not semantically meaningful

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under shuffling of SQ header record subtags, as subtag order within SQ records is not semantically significant

evidence.quote: SQ header record subtags are key-value pairs; their order is not semantically meaningful for queries

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under shuffling of RG header record subtags, as subtag order within RG records is not semantically significant

evidence.quote: RG header record subtags are key-value pairs; their order is not semantically meaningful for queries

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under shuffling of PG header record subtags, as subtag order within PG records is not semantically significant

evidence.quote: PG header record subtags are key-value pairs; their order is not semantically meaningful for queries

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under shuffling of CO comment lines, as comment order is not semantically significant

evidence.quote: CO header lines are comments; their order is not semantically meaningful for data queries

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under SUT write round trip, as writing and re-reading should preserve all data fields

evidence.quote: A write round-trip (serialize then deserialize) should preserve all semantically meaningful fields

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under a minor version bump in the HD header, as minor version changes indicate backward-compatible additions

evidence.quote: Minor version bumps in SAM indicate backward-compatible changes; query semantics should be preserved

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under removal of zero-length CIGAR operations, as they have no effect on alignment

evidence.quote: Zero-length CIGAR operations are no-ops; their removal does not change the alignment semantics

quarantine.oracle: Query results are invariant under stripping of mate-pair flags when reads are unpaired, as mate flags are meaningless for unpaired reads

evidence.quote: Mate-pair flags (0x1, 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80) are only meaningful for paired-end reads
